currentPosition.summary: Currently focused on stage 7 with 42 active tasks.

tasks.name: SPI 48 MHz support
tasks.description:
[@Kumaresh Dhotrad](#user_mention#89339454) can you describe what is required in terms of setup, and test support? .
**What & Why**
Faster SPI to enable higher throughput on QS222
**Implementation Plan**
*   update voltages LDO and SBB2 in the board files for the
*   If the desired SPI frequency is 48 MHz, set the system clock to 20 MHz.

**Completion criteria**
The task creator should specify the completion criteria, including specific results and metrics in a set of bullet points.

comments.text: @Kumaresh Dhotrad closing, as I believe this is complete. There was some delay between shipping, receiving and testing wearables, and because the operating voltage for wearables is inconsistent.
comments.author: Suhaib Ishaque

comments.text:
Able to run per tests on Wearables at 48MHz SPI.

Issues:
On the standalone application the config and fifo r/w was happening at LDO1 voltage of 950. This had run for 40K iterations.
On the testbench application, I tested on 4 wearables available in BLR. 2 devices worked at LD01 voltage of 975mV for 10K iterations of config r/w and the other 2 worked at LDO1 voltage of 1000mV.
image.jpg
With Sumon's input, I have created a board file evk_v1_48mhz with an LDO1 voltage of 1050mv. On this device I was able to run per tests in QAM4. I have attached the config for the test run.
Voltages measured on devices in the working and non working case. Below is the tabulated results.image.jpg
QAM4_WearableSetup_Schedule_18.2.26_48MHz.zip
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

comments.text: @Kumaresh Dhotrad said he received the EVK hardware yesterday, and plans to debug first thing tomorrow.
comments.author: Suhaib Ishaque

comments.text:
@Kumaresh Dhotrad ok, a few follow up questions:
are setup conditions, the failure mode, expected and actual results documented anywhere?
who will be getting you the boards, when?
do you know who's defining the priority of this debug versus other work (on-chip ADR and FFT)
comments.author: Suhaib Ishaque

comments.text: @Suhaib Ishaque  Not yet. Tired running PER test on wearable, which did not succeed as expected. Need boards to verify this.
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

comments.text: @Kumaresh Dhotrad can we close this task?
comments.author: Suhaib Ishaque

comments.text: Created PR for SPI 48MHz on IC-testbench
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

comments.text:
Tested the 48MHz spi works for DAQ and EVK boards. 

For the voltage changes we decided to create a separate board file daqboard_48mhz for DAQ board which will have the voltage changes to support SPI at 48 MHz.

For the EVK, I have asked if we want to increase the drive strength of the SPI lines the evk_v1 board or this would also need a separate board file which can support SPI at 48MHz.

Made the necessary changes in IC-testbench and pushed it to SPI_48MHz_Support branch.
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

tasks.name: N3: SPI 48MHz debug phase 2.

comments.text:
For the voltage changes we decided to create a separate board file daqboard_48mhz for DAQ board which will have the voltage changes to support SPI at 48 MHz.

For the EVK, I have asked if we want to increase the drive strength of the SPI lines the evk_v1 board or this would also need a separate board file which can support SPI at 48MHz.

Made the necessary changes in IC-testbench and created PR SPI_48MHz_Support
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

comments.text:
We've run tests on wearables. On the wearables the tests run with the below are the lowest numbers that spi at 48MHz runs on 
 
SBB2 2200
LDO1 at 900

This test was also run for 40000 iterations.
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

comments.text:
48MHz SPI works with the following changes on DAQ boards:

There were a couple of issues:
The IO(2.2V), VDD0P8(0.95V) both supplies needed to increased to 2.4V and 1.15V. 
The default sys clk at 10MHz needed to changed to 20MHz at lower SPI freq and then the freq needs to be changed to 48MHz for it work. Previously I had missed this part of setting the sys clk at lower SPI freq.
Test that we ran:
reset ic
set spi freq to 9.6MHz
set sys clk to 20MHz 
set spi freq to 48MHz
Write the config
Read config
Write 4096 bytes of data to FIFO
Read 4096 bytes of data back and validate
This test was run for 40000 iterations and it passed every time.
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

comments.text:
Write is working read is not  
write to mux_sel0 toggles the clk which can be observed on waveforms
write to ps_cfg_bypass_en_fe_ldo toggles voltages on VDD_FE which was measured on multimeter
write to cfg_rxpll_ldo_entoggles voltages on VDD_FE which was measured on multimeter
Read is not working 
We write 0xf to mux_sel0  to on the clock, read after write returns 0
Tried writing 1st byte of every bank to 0xff and read. Most of the bytes are 0 some bytes return junk value
image.jpg
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

tasks.name: N3: RX and TX PLL settling time
tasks.description:
1. measure the rx and tx\_pll settling time . final frequency will be 5Mhz

use the rx/tx pll settings from rx/tx pll jitter and frequency offset file.
enable te rx pll using
1. ps\_cfg\_direct\_en\_rx\_pll
2. ps\_cfg\_bypass\_en\_rx\_pll = make it 0 to 1

enable te tx pll using
1. ps\_cfg\_direct\_en\_tx\_pll
2. ps\_cfg\_bypass\_en\_tx\_pll = make it 0 to 1

design doc : ![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/977549bf-74e4-4d15-aaf6-d6413d641ac8/image.png)

Goal: The idea of the

Test Results : [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/PLL\_Test/QS222\_PLL\_Frequency\_Results.pptx?d=w652b8d8a8b3148b393a1841557506034&csf=1&web=1&e=sAdnMp&nav=eyJzSWQiOjU3NywiY0lkIjozMTgxNzc4MTQyfQ](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/PLL_Test/QS222_PLL_Frequency_Results.pptx?d=w652b8d8a8b3148b393a1841557506034&csf=1&web=1&e=sAdnMp&nav=eyJzSWQiOjU3NywiY0lkIjozMTgxNzc4MTQyfQ)

comments.text: @Deepthi Kammath @sumon  - As per discussion with Sumon, Deepthi, Sarat - and based on the design review - the RX PLL settings (divider should be 32) - to get 5 MHz feedback clock. The settling time is around 23 us and the design table - may not have captured the settling time for all possible RX PLL targeted frequency outputs. So, the comparing 23us of settling time with the data presented in the table may not be accurate. Hence for now, team will accept the settling time as it is and new task will be planned to measure the settling time for the various RX PLL intended frequencies.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: @Sumon - TX PLL, RX PLL - are the 2 different instations of the same PLL design and hence the results are same for both the Tx and Rx PLL.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: @sumon  Results are added in the description and results have been reviewed with Arka and the settling time measured is as expected.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: @Deepthi Kammath please add your results in the description section
comments.author: Pushpal Das

comments.text: Test Results : 10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/PLL_Test/QS222_PLL_Frequency_Results.pptx?d=w652b8d8a8b3148b393a1841557506034&csf=1&web=1&e=sAdnMp&nav=eyJzSWQiOjU3NywiY0lkIjozMTgxNzc4MTQyfQ
comments.author: Deepthi Kammath

tasks.blockerReason:
Delay Reason @pm:
1. Summary: The settling time of the RX PLL was not accurately captured in the design table.
2. Technical: Insufficient detail in thread regarding the exact settling times for different RX PLL targeted frequency outputs.

tasks.name: N3: [RX Validation] QAM16 PMDR across packet sizes (32,64,128,255,511,1023,1800) with and without ECC/CRC with SPI clock = 24MHz and System Clock = 10MHz
tasks.description:
**Why:** QS222 - RX testing needs to be validated and proven working in schedule mode for wearable deployment.
DR\_Scale : 0 (Focus is to get the sensitivity curves plotted for DR scale 0; other DR\_Scales will be addressed later).
**Deliverable:** Get the sensitivity curves (PMDR - vs input amp) with error correcting mechanisms enabled (ECC, CRC) for various packet sizes (32, 64, 128, 255, 511, 1023, 1800) in QAM16 mode. Given the known issue of 100% PMDR spikes w.r.t input amp - the sensitivity curves might not match the design expectations.
Hence the deliverable for this task is to get the sensitivity curves plot (as it is) and as a stretch goal to complete some debug cycles for a given packet size to improve the sensitivity curve.

**For Debug:** Engineer can pick a packet size that can be used as a best case study for debug studies based on the data collection across the different packet sizes.

**Risks (T,E):**
(T) - A known issue with 100% PMDR is being debugged in parallel for QAM16, packet size 32, without error correction mechanisms by Vinayak.
(T) - no known configuration settings yet that are proven to provide the sensitivity curves as per design expectation.

**Limitations:** (Assumptions (A), Constraints (C), Dependency(D))
A: No further surprises in the sensitivity curves surfaces beyond a known issue (100% PMDR spikes) after enabling correction mechanisms.
C: so far no known constraints from lab equipment side.

**Results:**
Deepthi will post the results here after getting the plots.

Results PPT: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Testing/RX\_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=rrkurP&nav=eyJzSWQiOjYzOCwiY0lkIjo1NDI4NjM2Nzl9](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Testing/RX_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=rrkurP&nav=eyJzSWQiOjYzOCwiY0lkIjo1NDI4NjM2Nzl9)
Conclusion: Results are matching with results from 9.6MHz SPI clock.
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/69a4a417-f341-4a7c-baf4-c9af6a5e2bf3/image.png)

comments.text: Hi @Pushpal Das , please note that this task was completed on time but closed later.
comments.author: Deepthi Kammath

comments.text: Thanks Deepthi.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Hi @Sarat Anumula, this task is complete and the results are updated in the description. Can you please close this task?
comments.author: Deepthi Kammath

Hence the deliverable for this task is to get the sensitivity curves plot (as it is) and as a stretch goal to complete some debug cycles for a given packet size to improve the sensitivity curve.

**For Debug:** Engineer can pick a packet size that can be used as a best case study for debug studies based on the data collection across the different packet sizes.

**Risks (T,E):**
(T) - A known issue with 100% PMDR is being debugged in parallel for QAM16, packet size 32, without error correction mechanisms by Vinayak.
(T) - no known configuration settings yet that are proven to provide the sensitivity curves as per design expectation.

**Limitations:** (Assumptions (A), Constraints (C), Dependency(D))
A: No further surprises in the sensitivity curves surfaces beyond a known issue (100% PMDR spikes) after enabling correction mechanisms.
C: so far no known constraints from lab equipment side.

**Results:**
Deepthi will post the results here after getting the plots.

Modulation : QAM2, QAM4, QAM16, QAM64
Packet Sizes (Bytes) : 16,100, 200, 1000, 1800  
Header Type : Enabled,Disabled

comments.text:
bb_test_results_variable_rs_configs.xlsxI noticed something interesting during recent runs. When the supply voltage is low(0.8V) , I occasionally see a single packet error pop up. But if I rerun the same test, it usually passes — so it seems like a transient issue rather than something persistent.
For most cases, using a 10 ms packet interval works fine — everything passes consistently. However, in QAM2 mode, when I drop the interval to 5 ms, a few cases start to fail.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text: image.png
comments.author: Anish Saha

tasks.name: Adiabatic mode

comments.text:
@Sarat Anumula @Pushpal Das 
Task Update:  This task can now be considered as Completed and can be closed now.
1. Summary
The task is now considered completed. Following a thorough debugging phase, all previously failing test cases have been resolved. The primary fix involved increasing the IDLE slot duration. Validation confirms that all cases now return pmdr = 0.
---2. Root Cause Analysis (RCA)
Two primary timing issues were identified as the causes of the failures:
MCU Packet Queuing Latency: Previously, MCU packets were not being queued quickly enough to meet system requirements. By adding sufficient IDLE slots, the timing bottleneck was eliminated, allowing the queuing process to complete without errors.
IRQ1 Clearing & BER Check Window: The original IDLE slot duration was insufficient for the Bit Error Rate (BER) check to finish. This prevented IRQ1 from clearing within the required timing window. Increasing the slot duration provided the necessary buffer for the BER check to conclude, resolving the timing conflict.
---3. Final Status
Multi-Packet: Validated (QAM, DSSS, and Adiabatic).
Success Metric: pmdr = 0 across all tested configurations.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:

Mode 2: CRC Only (CRC: On, RS: Off)​ ​
Mode 3: CRC + RS (CRC: On, RS: On)

undefinedValidation Summary (MAX Throughput Observed)​
For QAM‑4 with 1010 B packets(4 Transactions), no CRC/RS‑CRC enabled, and a 3.5 ms, the maximum achievable throughput under this spec is 7.48 MBps ​
comments.author: Anish Saha

For QAM‑2 with 1010 B packets (4 Transactions), no CRC/RS‑CRC enabled, and a 6.75 ms active slot , the maximum achievable throughput under this spec is 4.26 MBps.

undefinedPayload Limit: When RS is enabled, the test validates both 127- and 255-byte payload limits. Other modes strictly use 255 byte .​
The test scales the transaction count inversely to the packet size (e.g., small packets like 16 bytes use up to 136 transactions, while large packets like 1800 bytes use only 1 or 2).
Validation Summary (MAX Throughput Observed)​
For QAM‑2 with 1351 B packets (3 Transactions), no CRC/RS‑CRC enabled, and a 26 ms active slot , the maximum achievable throughput under this spec is 1.17 MBps.
comments.author: Anish Saha

tasks.name: N3: miso pin activity reduction  dueing continuous transmission
tasks.description:
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/890f49a4-98d0-4160-a71e-8fea6c841612/image.png)
\]
If the TX  is transmitting, then it can happen. 
I am assuming this is the en\_encoder = 0 mode. Then the TX starts transmitting while the SPI is still writing the packet.
In that case the register that the MISO line reads from (Basically same as the SRAM address the PHY is reading from), keeps changing and so the MISO line keeps toggling

tasks.description:
This is an extension to the previous debug studies of temp sensor : N3: \[Misc\_test\] TEMPARATURE SENSOR TEST ([https://app.clickup.com/t/86dy17k0m](https://app.clickup.com/t/86dy17k0m))

**Why:** Ixana's QS222 - have a temp sensor that needs to be validated for the proper functioning of the QS222 product.
**Deliverable:** Measure the temperature analog value and digital code in analog and digital path
**Risks (T,E):** Risk: T- If it does not function as intended, we may need to proceed with a re-tapeout.
**Limitations: (Assumptions(A), Constraints (C), Dependency )**
C: Constraint: Since Ixana post silicon lab doesn't have means to test at the other temperatures - it is assumed that the temp sensor readings from QS222 at room temp is good enough indicator for the working of the temp sensor.
A: Assumption- Setup and config are correct

tasks.name: N3: QS222 PMU TEST
tasks.description:
Deliverable:
docs:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-8f7d35b9-541e-4c20-abb9-d0712481e839)
1. **CHIP\_EN**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
2. **VDDIO\_ANA**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
3. **VDDIO\_AON**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
4. **VDDIO\_BUCK**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
5. **VDDIO\_TX**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
6. **VDDIO\_TXANA**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
7. BYPASS\_PS = 2.2 (IO voltage)
8. EN\_EFUSERD\_N = 2.2V (IO voltage) (For non-efuse read bootup)
9. Check the supplies at:
    1. **BUCK\_OUT\_1P2** pin (1.2V)
    2. **BUCK\_0P8 = 1V**
    3. Signals at these ports indicate dc-dc and vdd\_aon working.
OBSERVATION: Check the BUCK\_OUT\_1P2 and BUCK\_0P8 pin.
expectation - BOUT\_OUT\_1P2 will be 1.2V and BUCK\_0P8 will be 1V.
Measurement: Vary the load current of 0.8V and 1.2V output and measure the efficiency of the dc-dc converter.

Capture the result in a slide and the slide here.

Risk: NA
Limitation: Assumption: input is properly given

**Following are the bench observations:** [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/p/shovan/ER-FaF8qVl1PmL2ByuSvNDcBDHs77eTIqGqKNbDvZSav3A?e=haK0HB](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/p/shovan/ER-FaF8qVl1PmL2ByuSvNDcBDHs77eTIqGqKNbDvZSav3A?e=haK0HB)
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/c08ec2ef-b719-441f-aef4-28f34d5c5b82/image.png)
Default voltages from SIMO
1p2 = 1.17V
0p8 = 0.98V

in order to get close to 1.2V, 0.8V respectively, following config is used:

```yaml
buck_sel_rfb_dig_nvm: 11
buck_sel_rfb_ana_nvm: 6
```

Post layout sims give **84% efficiency** for 1p2Buck = 1.2V, 0p8Buck = 0.8V,
For the same scenario on bench we are getting **~79% efficiency**

**Further investigation to be performed**: [@sumon](#user_mention#89154620) can you mentioned steps for future

comments.text: Sumon: 72% efficiency was seen at first. Discussed with me and got the correct measurement
comments.author: Pushpal Das

5. **VDDIO\_TX**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
6. **VDDIO\_TXANA**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
7. BYPASS\_PS = 2.2 (IO voltage)
8. EN\_EFUSERD\_N = 2.2V (IO voltage) (For non-efuse read bootup)
9. Check the supplies at:
    1. **BUCK\_OUT\_1P2** pin (1.2V)
    2. **BUCK\_0P8 = 1V**
    3. Signals at these ports indicate dc-dc and vdd\_aon working.
OBSERVATION: Check the BUCK\_OUT\_1P2 and BUCK\_0P8 pin.
expectation - BOUT\_OUT\_1P2 will be 1.2V and BUCK\_0P8 will be 1V.
Measurement: Vary the load current of 0.8V and 1.2V output and measure the efficiency of the dc-dc converter.

Capture the result in a slide and the slide here.

Risk: NA
Limitation: Assumption: input is properly given

**Following are the bench observations:** [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/p/shovan/ER-FaF8qVl1PmL2ByuSvNDcBDHs77eTIqGqKNbDvZSav3A?e=haK0HB](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/p/shovan/ER-FaF8qVl1PmL2ByuSvNDcBDHs77eTIqGqKNbDvZSav3A?e=haK0HB)
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/c08ec2ef-b719-441f-aef4-28f34d5c5b82/image.png)
Default voltages from SIMO
1p2 = 1.17V
0p8 = 0.98V

in order to get close to 1.2V, 0.8V respectively, following config is used:

```yaml
buck_sel_rfb_dig_nvm: 11
buck_sel_rfb_ana_nvm: 6
```

Post layout sims give **84% efficiency** for 1p2Buck = 1.2V, 0p8Buck = 0.8V,
For the same scenario on bench we are getting **~79% efficiency**

**Further investigation to be performed**: [@sumon](#user_mention#89154620) can you mentioned steps for future
raw.creator: sumon

comments.text: Sumon: 72% efficiency was seen at first. Discussed with me and got the correct measurement

tasks.name: N3: Adiabatic QAM4 testing varying packet sizes, DR_scale

comments.text:
@Sarat Anumula @Pushpal Das 
The task was completed ahead of the due date, but since the review was still in progress, it was closed at a later time.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:
@Sarat Anumula 
QAM 4 / Packet 16 / No Encoding: DR_Scale 2 was skipped. (Reason:  TX does not transmit packet)
QAM 4 / Packet 16 / CRC & RS _Enabled (127): DR_Scale 1 was skipped.  (Reason:  TX does not transmit packet)

The above issues appear to have been a one-time occurrence. After repeating the experiment, we did not encounter any problems while performing the test in that particular configuration. Therefore, we do not need to create a separate debug task for it, as the issue is no longer present.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:
Missing QAM4 Cases (Corner Cases):
QAM 4 / Packet 16 / No Encoding: DR_Scale 2 was skipped. (Reason:  TX does not transmit packet)
QAM 4 / Packet 16 / CRC & RS _Enabled (127): DR_Scale 1 was skipped.  (Reason:  TX does not transmit packet)
QAM 4 / For Larger packets higher dr_scales (11,12) were skipped (Reason:  Active slot duration becoming very large,).

tasks.name: N3: IRQ bug resolve to get higher throughput

comments.text: Functionality was added on Mar 17th and verified on the following day. Hence delay of 2 days.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Added the functionality to handle pending IRQ in the WiR driver
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

comments.text: @sumon @Sarat Anumula  we need to change the due date
comments.author: Pushpal Das

comments.text:
Attempted to resolve the IRQ bug and tried coule of scenarios. 

Packet size of 1000 bytes at QAM64 with dr_scale 0. In this configuration, the IC works as expect and the pending IRQ gets cleared post handling the previous SPI transaction.
Packet size of 1800 bytes at QAM64 with dr_scale 0. In this configuration, the IC does not work as expected. The IC transmits the first packet correctly and the second packet is partially transmitted. Based on the inputs of @Sayantan Dey this happens because of a RTL bug and not much can be done in the firmware.
@Pushpal Das Reason for dealy:
Needed sometime figure out the root cause
Got stuck with higher priority tasks. 
This task will be taken up this week with throughput optimization.

tasks.name: N3: Adiabatic QAM2 testing varying packet sizes, DR_scale

comments.text:
@Sarat Anumula @Pushpal Das 
The task was completed ahead of the due date, but since the review was still in progress, it was closed at a later time.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:
@Sarat Anumula 
QAM 2 / Packet 64 B / CRC enabled, RS disbled , max payload : 127/255 : DR_Scale 0 was skipped. (Reason:  TX does not transmit packet)
QAM 2 / Packet 128 B/ CRC & RS _Enabled  , Max Payload : 255 : DR_Scale 0 was skipped.  (Reason:  TX does not transmit packet)

The above issues appear to have been a one-time occurrence. After repeating the experiment, we did not encounter any problems while performing the test in that particular configuration. Therefore, we do not need to create a separate debug task for it, as the issue is no longer present.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:
Missing QAM2 Cases (Corner Cases):
QAM 2 / Packet 64 B / CRC enabled, RS disbled , max payload : 127/255 : DR_Scale 0 was skipped. (Reason:  TX does not transmit packet)
QAM 2 / Packet 128 B/ CRC & RS _Enabled  , Max Payload : 255 : DR_Scale 0 was skipped.  (Reason:  TX does not transmit packet)
QAM 2 / For Larger packets higher dr_scales (10,11,12) were skipped (Reason:  Active slot duration becoming very large,).
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:
Summary Table: QAM 2 (BPSK) Checked Configs
DR_Scale Range: Mostly 0–12, with truncation at larger packet sizes. (As active slot duration was becoming very large approx 2s )
 3 Modes : No Encoding, CRC enabled, CRC & RS enabled.

undefined

tasks.description:

tasks.name: N3: [Other_Misc] Removal of buck_low_pwr_cfg_bits as reserved and making as accessible from GUI (enhancement to Python and excel change)
tasks.description:
**Why:** Config bits needs to have a mean to access by internal engineering teams for thorough validation of the QS222.

**Deliverable:** Need to make enhancements to presets to make the buck\_low\_pwr\_cfg\_bits to be able to access them. Currently this register is defined as reserved and hence the tool cannot access this register. Current workaround used by the team is to access them via SPI read/ write.

Required
0x0009: buck\_sel\_vref\_cntrl\_lp: bit\_width = 4: bit\_offset = 3
0x0013: buck\_sel\_rfb\_dig\_lp: bitt\_width = 4: bit\_offset = 0
0x0013: buck\_sel\_rfb\_ana\_lp: bitt\_width = 4: bit\_offset = 4

**Risks: (t)** - Limiting the accessibility to the config registers may not help internal product engineering team to thoroughly validate it.

comments.text: Thanks @Abhishek Sarkar
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
Changes done in presets and config sheet.
TBD- sanity check by running the GUI with the update and then push to git. @Vinayak Agrawal will check this.
comments.author: Abhishek Sarkar

1. Summary: The task to make buck_low_pwr_cfg_bits accessible from GUI was delayed due to insufficient technical details provided by internal engineering teams.

tasks.name: N1: [Baseband] FIFO read write testing for an extended period (10-15 days)
tasks.description:
**Why:** To thoroughly validate FIFO read, write operation and confirm it is working over an extended period of time. Prior generation product QS127S has a known bit flipping issue during read/ write operation.

**Deliverable:** a) Capture the bit flipping from the FIFO read /write operation for an extended period of time (10-14 days) with external supply.
b) Check the FIFO read / write with internal supply.

**Risks(T,E):**
**Limitations (Assumptions (A), Constraints (C), Dependency(D))**
**C:** Need some additional J-Links for the setup for enhanced testing and remote controllability.

**Results:**
Anish will update the results upon test completion.

tasks.name: N3: [Other_Misc] MEASUREMENT FIFO VALIDATION - part1.
tasks.description:
**Why:** Measurement FIFO is a FIFO inside where all the configuration (limited to RX path) settings are stored for a given packet received. This measurement FIFO needs to be properly validated so as to make sure the IC is configured correctly for the signal detection (signal recovery).

**Deliverable:** Report all the heat maps of below variables w.r.t signal amplitude.
part1 variables:
pkt\_reject','pkt\_accept','desc\_idx','agc\_gain\_search','cqi\_done','agc\_done','cqi'

**Limitations:** (Assumptions(A), Constraints (C), Dependency(D).

**Results:** Anish will share the results here in comments or in the description.

tasks.name: N3: [RX validation] Debug the PMDR floor for data rate < 5Mbps without idle slots.
tasks.description:
Debug Results PPT: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Testing/RX\_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=b5gIsb&nav=eyJzSWQiOjcyNSwiY0lkIjoxMDQ5NTkwNzY2fQ](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Testing/RX_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=b5gIsb&nav=eyJzSWQiOjcyNSwiY0lkIjoxMDQ5NTkwNzY2fQ)

This issue looks like a f/w issue.
With packet interval = Active slot, we see two problems
1. We only see 650 number of IRQs out of 1000. By increasing the packet interval (i.e. adding idle slot), we do not see this problem and the IRQs are as expected.
2. The SPI readout is not happening randomly even though the IRQ was provided by the chip.

These issues are to be checked in the f/w and Kumaresh will take this up.

Please find the scope captures :

IRQ received only 650 times out of 1000:
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/fe2fd62a-9476-4a0d-95ea-07ad9dc880dd/image.png)

SPI readout not happening even though IRQ is present:
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/9fb8f69c-280d-465c-a0ed-b0e7d3aab0fb/image.png)

Resolution:
The issue was with the software where the wait time for 'test done' was being calculated in terms of the duration set in the GUI as below. 

wait\_for\_test\_done = duration \* pkt\_count + 5

So when we set the duration as 0 in the GUI for avoiding the idle slot, the wait time is 5sec (= 0 \* 1000 + 5). This time is not sufficient for lower data rates and hence the test was stopped before receiving all packets. 

We tested with the corrected code which takes in the IC interval (active slot + idle slot) instead of GUI duration setting. This resolved the issue. 

wait\_for\_test\_done = **IC interval** \* pkt\_count + 5

comments.author: Sarat Anumula

raw.name: N3: [RX validation] Debug the PMDR floor for data rate < 5Mbps without idle slots.
raw.description:
Debug Results PPT: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Testing/RX\_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=b5gIsb&nav=eyJzSWQiOjcyNSwiY0lkIjoxMDQ5NTkwNzY2fQ](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Testing/RX_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=b5gIsb&nav=eyJzSWQiOjcyNSwiY0lkIjoxMDQ5NTkwNzY2fQ)

This issue looks like a f/w issue.
With packet interval = Active slot, we see two problems
1. We only see 650 number of IRQs out of 1000. By increasing the packet interval (i.e. adding idle slot), we do not see this problem and the IRQs are as expected.
2. The SPI readout is not happening randomly even though the IRQ was provided by the chip.

These issues are to be checked in the f/w and Kumaresh will take this up.

Please find the scope captures :

IRQ received only 650 times out of 1000:
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/fe2fd62a-9476-4a0d-95ea-07ad9dc880dd/image.png)

SPI readout not happening even though IRQ is present:
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/9fb8f69c-280d-465c-a0ed-b0e7d3aab0fb/image.png)

Resolution:
The issue was with the software where the wait time for 'test done' was being calculated in terms of the duration set in the GUI as below. 

wait\_for\_test\_done = duration \* pkt\_count + 5

So when we set the duration as 0 in the GUI for avoiding the idle slot, the wait time is 5sec (= 0 \* 1000 + 5). This time is not sufficient for lower data rates and hence the test was stopped before receiving all packets. 

We tested with the corrected code which takes in the IC interval (active slot + idle slot) instead of GUI duration setting. This resolved the issue. 

wait\_for\_test\_done = **IC interval** \* pkt\_count + 5
raw.creator: Sarat Anumula

tasks.name: Resolve the discrepancies seen in the active slot numbers from the calculation and that from the oscilloscope measurements
tasks.description:
Test Results: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Testing/RX\_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=oQ4YFh&nav=eyJzSWQiOjY4MSwiY0lkIjoxMzcyMDEzNDQ4fQ](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Testing/RX_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=oQ4YFh&nav=eyJzSWQiOjY4MSwiY0lkIjoxMzcyMDEzNDQ4fQ)

Summary:
The discrepancy was due to wrong calculation of the slot duration in the code

Scope Capture
Total packet duration = 885.6us

Code Calculation:
Total packet duration = 891.2us
This calculation is wrong due to the following
1.Thetxphy\_tx\_valid\_extra\_count was multiplied by 2 for I and Q channels. This is not to be done for end symbols
2.txphy\_tx\_margin\_cycles was added to calculate the end symbols. This is not to be added.

After correcting the above in the slot duration calculation, the numbers are matching with what we measure in the scope

**Packetduration=885.6us**
**RXdecode+transaction end + slot end = 118.4us**
**Total active slot duration =1.004us**

comments.text: Thanks Deepthi.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Hi @Sarat Anumula, this task is completed. Can you please close the task?
comments.author: Deepthi Kammath

comments.text:
QAM‑64 First Debug Cycle – Summary of Configurations Tested : 

Link to Excel :  

Mode 1: Raw (CRC: Off, RS: Off)​ ​
Mode 2: CRC Only (CRC: On, RS: Off)​ ​
Mode 3: CRC + RS (CRC: On, RS: On)
Transaction Counts: Number of Packets per slot.​
 
 

undefined

Payload Limit: When RS is enabled, the test validates both 127- and 255-byte payload limits. Other modes strictly use 255 byte .​
The test scales the transaction count inversely to the packet size (e.g., small packets like 16 bytes use up to 136 transactions, while large packets like 1800 bytes use only 1 or 2).

Validation Summary (MAX Throughput Observed)​
For QAM‑64 with 1010 byte packets(4 Transactions), no CRC/RS‑CRC enabled,and a 1.3 ms active slot & 0.833  ms idle slot, the maximum achievable throughputunder this spec is 15.15 Mbps. (Ideal = 24.86 Mbps)
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:

Transaction Counts: Number of Packets per slot.​ 

undefined

Payload Limit: When RS is enabled, the test validates both 127- and 255-byte payload limits. Other modes strictly use 255 byte .​
The test scales the transaction count inversely to the packet size (e.g., small packets like 16 bytes use up to 136 transactions, while large packets like 1800 bytes use only 1 or 2).

Validation Summary (MAX Throughput Observed)​
For QAM‑16 with 1010 B packets(4 Transactions), no CRC/RS‑CRC enabled, and a1.85 ms active slot & 0.833 ms idle slot, the maximum achievable throughputunder this spec is 12.05 Mbps. (Ideal = 17.5 Mbps)
comments.author: Anish Saha

QAM‑4 First Debug Cycle – Summary of Configurations Tested : 
Link to Excel :  

Mode 1: Raw (CRC: Off, RS: Off)​ ​
Mode 2: CRC Only (CRC: On, RS: Off)​ ​
Mode 3: CRC + RS (CRC: On, RS: On)
Transaction Counts: Number of Packets per slot.​

 

undefined 

Payload Limit: When RS is enabled, the test validates both 127- and 255-byte payload limits. Other modes strictly use 255 byte .​
The test scales the transaction count inversely to the packet size (e.g., small packets like 16 bytes use up to 136 transactions, while large packets like 1800 bytes use only 1 or 2).

Validation Summary (MAX Throughput Observed)​
For QAM‑4 with 1010 B packets(4 Transactions), no CRC/RS‑CRC enabled, and a 3.5 ms active slot & 0.833 ms idle slot, the maximum achievable throughput underthis spec is 7.47 Mbps. (Ideal = 9.25 Mbps)

tasks.name: N3: [RX Validation] QAM16 - Data collection to help with RX PMDR optimization
tasks.description:
**Why:** Data collection is needed to help the RX PMDR optimization for QS222 characterization.

**Deliverable:**
Get the data in QAM 16 mode with two different power supplies - and do a comparative and contrast studies for best settings for an optimized EVM.

**Risks:** (T) - Leveraging the work from previous mode - may not be a straightforward approach.

**Limitations** (Assumptions (A), Constraints (C), Dependency (D) ) :
(A) - the scripts will work as is for QAM16 mode without any changes.

**Results:**
Deepthi will post the results here after task is completed.

Test results PPT: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Rampup/RX\_Ramp%20up.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=Cemqec&nav=eyJzSWQiOjU4OSwiY0lkIjoyMDQ5Mzc4OTN9](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Rampup/RX_Ramp%20up.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=Cemqec&nav=eyJzSWQiOjU4OSwiY0lkIjoyMDQ5Mzc4OTN9)

tasks.name: N3: [PLL Validation] RX PLL settling time for multiple carrier frequencies.
tasks.description:
**Why:** Characterization of the RX PLL of all the supported frequencies is required for better study and feedback on the receiver which in turn can help the system level testing of QS 222.

**Deliverable:** Capture the settling time for all the multiple possible carrier frequencies for which the receiver is designed to operate with.

**Required Documents:**
_PLL target frequencies_: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/g/personal/arka\_chowdhury\_ixana\_ai/EdK9sEv8f7tOh-pyTW\_0CqIBaQroO3xqOEk-dQILW8FAMg?wdOrigin=TEAMS-MAGLEV.p2p\_ns.rwc&wdExp=TEAMS-TREATMENT&wdhostclicktime=1762920015857&web=1](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/g/personal/arka_chowdhury_ixana_ai/EdK9sEv8f7tOh-pyTW_0CqIBaQroO3xqOEk-dQILW8FAMg?wdOrigin=TEAMS-MAGLEV.p2p_ns.rwc&wdExp=TEAMS-TREATMENT&wdhostclicktime=1762920015857&web=1)
_config\_qs\_222.xls_: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/\_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BEBE8AE7B-817C-4527-92E3-7EE913D31A0A%7D&file=config\_qs222.xlsx&action=default&mobileredirect=true](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BEBE8AE7B-817C-4527-92E3-7EE913D31A0A%7D&file=config_qs222.xlsx&action=default&mobileredirect=true) Register fields information.
_test description web page:_ [](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?block=block-744871b0-e275-40b0-a87b-280381547436)(Refer to the section of the PLL Tests for test description)

**Risk:**
**Limitations - Assumption:**
1.Ratio (divider) settings for the various RX PLL frequencies are accurate and work as expected.
2\. Front end enabling details (are needed) for RX-PLL and RX PLL output monitor ( works as expected in the lab.
**Limitations - Constraints:**
**Limitations - Dependencies:**
If the above assumptions are not working as planned, additional debug is needed.
Control bits (**_ps\_dr\_idx\_rx\_0\_carr\_sel_**) - need to be programmed appropriately for each targeted frequency of the RX PLL.

**Results: (**Results will be added later by Deepthi after the task is complete).
Capture all the test details and results here in this page: PLL TX RX - Validation ([https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-73137](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-73137))

Test Results : [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/PLL\_Test/QS222\_PLL\_Tests.pptx?d=w652b8d8a8b3148b393a1841557506034&csf=1&web=1&e=uxrL0V&nav=eyJzSWQiOjU4NiwiY0lkIjoyNTU2ODg4OTcwfQ](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/PLL_Test/QS222_PLL_Tests.pptx?d=w652b8d8a8b3148b393a1841557506034&csf=1&web=1&e=uxrL0V&nav=eyJzSWQiOjU4NiwiY0lkIjoyNTU2ODg4OTcwfQ)

1.Ratio (divider) settings for the various RX PLL frequencies are accurate and work as expected.

**Limitations - Constraints:**
**Limitations - Dependencies:**
If the above assumptions are not working as planned, additional debug is needed.

comments.text:
Debug Update – 17th Nov
Unable to run tests for packet sizes beyond 1792 bytes.
Root cause: max_buffer_size was set to 1800, which limited usable packet size to 1792 bytes (excluding header).
Fix: Increased max_buffer_size to 1814 bytes, allowing previously failing test cases to run successfully.
Next step: Tomorrow, I will debug the rs_crc-enabled failures together to identify the root cause.

tasks.name: N3: [Baseband] Base band testing in schedule mode with IDLE slots.
tasks.description:
doc:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-c9ca6436-5131-4f03-8e8f-38bf36240230)
deliverable:

SPI = 9.6M, SYSCLK= 10M, 5M, RUN WITH internal simo - lower the 1V supply to 0.8. if 0.8V does not work , check at 0.85, 100 packets - header on. a) no encoding, b) crc, c) rs+crc, max payload size 255 and max parity 32, Header RS Parity = 3(16 Bytes)
1. QAM 2- packet size - 1 to1800 (In steps of 10), dr\_scale = 0 to 6 in step of 1
2. QAM 4- packet size - 1 to1800 (In steps of 10), dr\_scale = 0 to 6 in step of 1
3. QAM 16- packet size - 1 to1800 (In steps of 10), dr\_scale = 0 to 6 in step of 1
4. QAM 64- packet size - 1 to1800 (In steps of 10), dr\_scale = 0 to 6 in step of 1

This will be the first debug cycle. Run the test and report the bug. no need to debug. will create a separate task for debug.
Risk: NA
Limitation: Assumption: FW and config settings are correct

comments.text: Modified the description to capture the values of the variable of the "packet size" accurately.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Changed the title to be more descriptive based on the actual lab validation done. (related to the IDLE slots).l
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: image.png
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:
Debug Results: 
The earlier slot duration calculation was not correct.​
At higher data‑rate scales, the RS decoder needed more cycles, and timing became too tight. 
Added Extra Cycles for Margin
All the Experiments are performed with IDLE slots.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text: @Anish Saha  - can you please include the debug notes (with Sayatan) here in comments and results in the description section of this task.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Changed the dates of start and end date.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
@Pushpal Das Reason for Delay:
 The slot timing calculations on QS222 were implemented incorrectly in the script. We received the official documentation for slot timing calculations yesterday, and we are currently applying it to the script. We expect this update to resolve the previously reported issues.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:
Current Problems observed in the first debug cycle.
Problem #1(Fixed)
Unable to run tests for packet sizes beyond 1792 bytes.​
Root cause: On FW max_buffer_size was set to 1800, which limited usable packet size to 1792 bytes(excluding header).​
Fix: Increased max_buffer_size to 1814 bytes, allowing previously failing test cases to run successfully​
Problem #2
RS + CRC enabled, getting unexpected results​
Complete description of the issue : 61597?block=block-0b42d49d-a301-4679-977d-e4f5e8db6414
Problem #3​
DR_Scale > 2, QAM4,QAM16,QAM64 not working.​
QAM2 1800 Packet Size Higher DR_Scale not working.​
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text: Assigned the owner as Anish Saha for all the sub tasks.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Updated the title - just included the name [Tx to Rx] - as we plan to streamline the tasks into the various vertical executions swimlanes.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

measure the Throughput - vs distance ;
measure PMDR - vs distance curves on different device pairs.

tasks.name: N3: [RX Validation] RX Validation in QAM64 mode. Path clearing.
tasks.description:
**Why:** QS 222 needs to be up and running in the schedule mode - this is the POR for the product deliverable. This is the most important test that we need to have it up and running for the QS222 for RX validation.

**Description:**
Mode : QAM 64,

**Deliverable:** Gather the feedback from the QS222 RX verification (operation) when run in the schedule mode. Metrics is the just packet reception (no EVM , no PMDR) -- an indication of Silicon being alive. Idea is to have this packet reception working on one device at one temp.

**Limitations:**
(D) Need design , Firmware, help from Sayantan , Souptik, Kumaresh to debug any issues surface during the Rx validation.

**Results:**
Vinayak - will post the links to the results either here in the description or in the comments section.

**Issues surfaced:**
Any issues surfaced /any new limitations surfaced will be documented here. Also any dependencies from any other teams will be documented here.

comments.text: For other DR scale - a new task will be created with phase 2 title. for multiple DR scales and phase 3 for the different carrier frequencies.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: This task is completed for path finding work for the QAM 64 model . DRScale 0.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: also, it was decided that Vinayak will work on the QS127m Power measurement scripts. Hence this task will be on hold and will resume work on this task.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

Notes from 23-Mar - Silicon results review meeting:

QAM4   config bit seems to have an undesired effect on the QAM64 performance (setup   related). Sensitivity improved from 1.2mV to 800uV by lowering the thresholds   for error correction for 13byte packet.

Next steps: Debug the 100% PMDR spikes

tasks.name: N3: [RX Validation] QAM16 Characterization. Multiple DR_scale, on chip supply, ADC clock scaling
tasks.description:
**Why:** Task is being explored to save power - by reducing the frequency of ADC (Analog to Digital Converter). **Deliverable:** Get the PMDR results w.r.t ADC clock frequency . Idea is to have the best PMDR at the lowest ADC clock. **Risks: N/A** **Limitations:** a) This task is being done with onboard supply. On chip supply still has few issues that needs to be resolved. b) Work arounds need to be used to make progress on this task (till these fixes are available in the released GUI). Results PPT: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Testing/RX\_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=aurfFP&nav=eyJzSWQiOjcxNCwiY0lkIjoxNDk1NDQ5ODcwfQ](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Testing/RX_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=aurfFP&nav=eyJzSWQiOjcxNCwiY0lkIjoxNDk1NDQ5ODcwfQ) Summary: The RX PMDR results are as expected and is working for dr scales with the lower ADC clocks as per the below table. DR scale 3 (2.5Mbps) requires additional Idle slot as PMDR floor around 30% was observed with only Active slot. This issue is debugged separately in N3: \[RX validation\] Debug the PMDR floor for data rate < 5Mbps without idle slots. ([https://app.clickup.com/t/86dzvmdhp](https://app.clickup.com/t/86dzvmdhp)) |
DR Scale
|

|

tasks.name: N3:[Baseband] Baseband testing - QAM, DSSS, Adiabatic in schedule mode with multi packets with & without Acks.
tasks.description:
doc:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-c9ca6436-5131-4f03-8e8f-38bf36240230)

**Why:** Need to have QS222 validated in the baseband testing in Schedule mode with Multiple Packets in a single slot for a thorough validation of QS222.

**Deliverable:**
Conditions: SPI -24 MHz, SYSCLK= 10 MHz ; Onboard supply.
500 packets - header on. a) no encoding, b) crc, c) rs+crc, max payload size 255, 127, and max parity 32, Header RS Parity = 1
1. QAM 2- packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 3 in step of 1
2. QAM 4- packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 4 in step of 1
3. QAM 16- packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 5 in step of 1
4. QAM 64- packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 5 in step of 1
5. DSSS QAM2 (BPSK) - packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800, dr\_scale = 3,4
6. DSSS QAM4 (BPSK) - packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800, dr\_scale = 3,4
7. Adiabatic QAM2 (BPSK) - packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800, dr\_scale = 0 to 3 in step of 1
8. Adiabatic QAM4 (QPSK) - packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800, dr\_scale = 0 to 4 in step of 1

**Limitations:** We need to create the multipacket transmission script on both FW and SW before validating transmission in a single slot.

**Results:** Anish will provide an update here (link to the results) after validation is completed.

comments.text:
Task is completed yesterday  (28-jan); addressed the feedback and provided the data requested by Shovan. 

Task was completed with one day delay.  Delay was mainly because of some unexpected issues surfaced and debug took time. (this was highlighted earlier as well). 

Continuous improvement:  Need to be more diligent in capturing the assumptions and limitations during the task definition stage.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
@Sarat Anumula @Pushpal Das 
Task Update:  This task can now be considered as Completed and can be closed now.
1. Summary
The task is now considered completed. Following a thorough debugging phase, all previously failing test cases have been resolved. The primary fix involved increasing the IDLE slot duration. Validation confirms that all cases now return pmdr = 0.
---2. Root Cause Analysis (RCA)
Two primary timing issues were identified as the causes of the failures:
MCU Packet Queuing Latency: Previously, MCU packets were not being queued quickly enough to meet system requirements. By adding sufficient IDLE slots, the timing bottleneck was eliminated, allowing the queuing process to complete without errors.
IRQ1 Clearing & BER Check Window: The original IDLE slot duration was insufficient for the Bit Error Rate (BER) check to finish. This prevented IRQ1 from clearing within the required timing window. Increasing the slot duration provided the necessary buffer for the BER check to conclude, resolving the timing conflict.
---3. Final Status
Multi-Packet: Validated (QAM, DSSS, and Adiabatic).
Success Metric: pmdr = 0 across all tested configurations.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:
Hi @Sarat Anumula @Pushpal Das 
Task Update:
- The first debug cycle for all subtasks has been completed.
- Results and summaries have been documented in the comments of the respective subtasks.
- Across all configurations, approximately 10–20 cases failed and require further debugging.
- Recommendation: Create a separate debug task focused specifically on Multipacket Transmission in Schedule Mode.
comments.author: Anish Saha

tasks.name: N3:[Baseband] base band testing in schedule mode without IDLE slots. ADIABATIC
tasks.description:
doc:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-c9ca6436-5131-4f03-8e8f-38bf36240230)
deliverable:
Conditions: SPI -24 MHz, SYSCLK= 10 MHz ; Onboard supply.
100 packets - header on. a) no encoding, b) crc, c) rs+crc, max payload size 255, 127, and max parity 32, Header RS Parity = 1

1. ADIABATIC QAM 2- packet size - 16,64,255,512,1024,1800, dr\_scale = 0 to 12 in steps of 1
2. ADIABATIC QAM 4- packet size - 16,64,255,512,1024,1800, dr\_scale = 0 to 12 in steps of 1

This will be the first debug cycle. Run the test and report the bug. no need to debug. will create a separate task for debug.
**Risk:** NA
**Limitation:** Assumption: FW and config settings are correct
**Constraint:** If the active slot duration is greater than 2sec then, Transmitted packets are not being received. Suspicion is that FW is unable to handle that high slot duration. (might need more deep dive studies later for a better understanding).

Based on the limited testing done - here are the conditions (corner cases) where active slot duration > 2 sec:
a) QAM4 pkt size > 500 bytes. DR\_Scale = 12;
b) QAM2 pkt size > 250 bytes. DR\_Scale = 12

Dependency: Sayantan help is needed for debug of the Frame duration when IDLE slots are removed.

The task was completed ahead of the due date, but since the review was still in progress, it was closed at a later time.

Based on the limited testing done - here are the conditions (corner cases) where active slot duration > 2 sec: 
a) QAM4 pkt size > 500 bytes. DR_Scale = 12;

Delay Reason @pm:
1. Summary: Limited testing revealed a limitation in handling active slot durations greater than 2 seconds.
2. Technical: Insufficient testing and analysis of corner cases, specifically QAM4 packet sizes > 500 bytes and QAM2 packet sizes > 250 bytes with DR_Scale = 12.

raw.name: N3:[Baseband] base band testing in schedule mode without IDLE slots. ADIABATIC
raw.description:
doc:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-c9ca6436-5131-4f03-8e8f-38bf36240230)
deliverable:
Conditions: SPI -24 MHz, SYSCLK= 10 MHz ; Onboard supply.
100 packets - header on. a) no encoding, b) crc, c) rs+crc, max payload size 255, 127, and max parity 32, Header RS Parity = 1

1. ADIABATIC QAM 2- packet size - 16,64,255,512,1024,1800, dr\_scale = 0 to 12 in steps of 1
2. ADIABATIC QAM 4- packet size - 16,64,255,512,1024,1800, dr\_scale = 0 to 12 in steps of 1

This will be the first debug cycle. Run the test and report the bug. no need to debug. will create a separate task for debug.
**Risk:** NA
**Limitation:** Assumption: FW and config settings are correct
**Constraint:** If the active slot duration is greater than 2sec then, Transmitted packets are not being received. Suspicion is that FW is unable to handle that high slot duration. (might need more deep dive studies later for a better understanding).

Based on the limited testing done - here are the conditions (corner cases) where active slot duration > 2 sec:
a) QAM4 pkt size > 500 bytes. DR\_Scale = 12;
b) QAM2 pkt size > 250 bytes. DR\_Scale = 12

Dependency: Sayantan help is needed for debug of the Frame duration when IDLE slots are removed.
raw.creator: Sarat Anumula

tasks.name: N3: [RX validation] PER_FG bringup in schedule mode.
tasks.description:
**Why:** QS 222 needs to be up and running in the schedule mode - this is the POR for the product deliverable. This is the most important test that we need to have it up and running for the QS222 for RX validation.

**Description:**

**Deliverable:** Gather the feedback from the QS222 RX verification (operation) when run in the schedule mode. Metrics is the just packet reception (no EVM , no PMDR) -- an indication of Silicon being alive. Idea is to have this packet reception working on one device at one temp.

**Limitations:**
(D) Need design , Firmware, help from Sayantan , Souptik, Kumaresh to debug any issues surface during the Rx validation.

tasks.name: N3: [QS222] Get the datasheet ready - Version1 - on or before 24-Dec . [CES event-Jan6].
tasks.description:
**Why:** Ixana plans to provide a demo of QS222 - at CES in Jan 2026. Hence needed to have an initial version of the datasheet out for the product QS222.

**Deliverable:** Prepare the first version of the datasheet with the basic functionality proven working with QS222.
Idea is to prepare the data sheet to include the operating data rate, modes of transmission, power consumption. Include the packaging diagrams as well.

**Limitations: (A):** Hope to get the Tx, Rx, baseband validation completed with the internal supply (in schedule mode - stretch goal).

**Risks: (T) :**
Completion of the data collection on atleast 6 devices - any issues that surface might delay in the data collection and may not be complete and also may not be accurate to include in the datasheet for publication.

**Results:**
Sarat will provide the link to the completed pdf document here.

tasks.name: N3: EFUSE TEST
tasks.description:
**Why:** Need to validate the fuse read , write operation for proper functioning of the QS222.
**Deliverable:**
_Measured Parameters:_ Write the EFUSE module. Write the config bank using efuse. Read the config bank written by the EFUSE.
_Expectation:_ The Read data will be the same as the write data.

Test Description:
[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-a70748c6-ffc0-455c-8f87-a2af3b44117d)

**Risk: NA**
**Limitation:** _Assumption:_ The setup and FW are ready and correct

comments.text:
To verify the functionality, I attempted to write all 0’s. As expected, the read operation returned the previously stored value from permanent memory.
image.png
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:
The EFUSE test was successful. As shown, the first row represents the data written to the EFUSE, and the second row shows the data read back from the EFUSE. These values match as expected. The value 170 appears after 1 because it had already been set to 170 during previous debug sessions.
image.png
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text: The implementation details are documented here.
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

tasks.name: N1: [RX validation] Fully Functional QAM 16 - handover to wearables.
tasks.description:
**Why:** QS 222 needs to be up and running in the schedule mode - this is the POR for the product deliverable. This is the most important test that we need to have it up and running for the QS222 for RX validation.

**Description:**
Mode : QAM16.

**Deliverable:** Gather the feedback from the QS222 RX verification (operation) when run in the schedule mode. Metrics is the just packet reception (no EVM , no PMDR) -- an indication of Silicon being alive. Idea is to have this packet reception working on one device at one temp.

**Limitations:**
(D) Need design , Firmware, help from Sayantan , Souptik, Kumaresh to debug any issues surface during the Rx validation.

**Results:**
Vinayak - will post the links to the results either here in the description or in the comments section.

**Issues surfaced:**
Any issues surfaced /any new limitations surfaced will be documented here. Also any dependencies from any other teams will be documented here.

comments.text:
This task is complete. the last remaining subtask of "Debugging the sensitivity on QS222 wearables" - ownership has been transferred to Anish.

[From Vinayak:]
Debugging the sensitivity requires RSSI Offset Correction and other such metrices which are being implemented by Anish and Kumaresh right now

tasks.name: N3:[Baseband] base band testing in schedule mode without IDLE slots. DSSS
tasks.description:
doc:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-c9ca6436-5131-4f03-8e8f-38bf36240230)
deliverable:
Conditions: SPI -24 MHz, SYSCLK= 10 MHz ; Onboard supply.
100 packets - header on. a) no encoding, b) crc, c) rs+crc, max payload size 255, 127, and max parity 32, Header RS Parity = 1
1.DSSS QAM 2- packet size - 16,64,255,512,1024,1800, dr\_scale = 3 to 10 insteps to 1
2.DSSS QAM 4- packet size - 16,64,255,512,1024,1800, dr\_scale = 3 to 10 insteps to 1

This will be the first debug cycle. Run the test and report the bug. no need to debug. will create a separate task for debug.
Risk: NA
Limitation: Assumption: FW and config settings are correct
Dependency: Sayantan help is needed for debug of the Frame duration when IDLE slots are removed.

**Results Summary:**
All listed conditions were tested under the following setup:
*   **Config:** SPI = 24 MHz, SYSCLK = 10 MHz, onboard supply
*   **Packets:** 100 packets with header enabled
    *   Modes: (a) no encoding, (b) CRC, (c) RS+CRC
    *   Payload sizes: 255, 127; max parity = 32; Header RS Parity = 1
*   **DSSS QAM‑2:** Packet sizes 16, 64, 255, 512, 1024, 1800; `dr_scale` = 3→10 (step 1)
*   **DSSS QAM‑4:** Packet sizes 16, 64, 255, 512, 1024, 1800; `dr_scale` = 3→10 (step 1)
**Issue:**​​
For **QAM‑4, Packet Size = 16**, the configuration with **Full CRC + RS** fails whenusing a payload size of **255** (en\_crc = True, en\_rs\_crc = True,max\_payload = 255).​

We faced few other issues like partial packet transmission. that is captured in the documents attached.

comments.text:
@Pushpal Das @Sarat Anumula 
The task was completed ahead of the due date, but since the review was still in progress, it was closed at a later time.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:
@Sarat Anumula 
Issue #1 appears to have been a one-time occurrence. After repeating the experiment, we did not encounter any problems while performing the test in that particular configuration. Therefore, we do not need to create a separate debug task for it, as the issue is no longer present.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text: marking as complete.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Issue #1 - will be tracked as a seperate task asa bug corner case debug).
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Thanks @Anish Saha  - as discussed in the Silicon results review meeting on 5-Jan - it si confirmed that the issue #2 is a bug and it is fixed in QS223  and hence will drop any debug effort on that issue. Here is the note to the bug (from Sumon) :
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
Hi @Sarat Anumula Sure here are the details, 
Issue #1 QAM‑4, Packet Size = 16, CRC & RS enabled, max_payload = 255, dsss enabled

Issue #2 QAM‑2, Packet Size = 1800, CRC & RS disabled, dsss enabled:  Partial Packet Transmission.

Please let me know if you need more details regarding the bugs.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text: Thanks @Anish Saha - for the corner cases surfaced - can you please provide a clear title to the problem here -so that we can track it separately.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
@Sarat Anumula 
We can consider this task to be completed. Further debug will be done for few corner cases with @Sayantan Dey & @Souptik Dolui, Depending on their bandwidth.
comments.author: Anish Saha

tasks.name: N3: Base band offset correction test
tasks.description:
Step:
1. Get the offset voltage (difference between outp and outn) using the manual sweep of the code, and store the analog voltages in an Excel
2. Run the automatic offset correction loop and get the correction code
3. Obtain the analog voltage (difference between outp and outn) for the 0 code and the code for the minimum difference (between outp and outn voltages) in Excel. Also, calculate the voltage difference for the code obtained from automatic baseband offset correction.

test description:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-990d0740-8c72-400e-92c9-409ece0a8328)

Risk: T- If it does not function as intended, we may need to proceed with a re-tapeout. and need to force the offset manually.
Limitation: Assumption: The setup, code, and FW is ready
tasks.blockerReason:
Delay Reason @pm:
1. Summary: Insufficient detail in thread
2. Technical: Delayed due to lack of detailed comments and analysis from team members.

**Limitations:**

tasks.name: N3: [RX-Validation] CES - Initial RX POWER MEASUREMENT- NON SCHEDULE MODE
tasks.description:
**Why:** Ixana needs to get basic power measurements for the publication of the numbers in the data sheet before the CES event in United States (Jan 5th)

**Deliverable:**
Get the power measurements for the basic mode of the operation of QS222 in the non schedule mode.
QAM16, DR\_scale: 0; packet size : N/A.
Need to get a basic estimate of the order (like mA, uA,..) of the power consumption by QS222 for CES event.
Full in-depth studies of the power measurements across various operating modes (QAM/ Dr\_Scale/ packet sizes) will be done later and it will be tracked in a separate task.

**Test Description:** RX POWER MEASUREMENT ([https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-76577](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-76577))

raw.name: N3: [RX-Validation] CES - Initial RX POWER MEASUREMENT- NON SCHEDULE MODE
raw.description:
**Why:** Ixana needs to get basic power measurements for the publication of the numbers in the data sheet before the CES event in United States (Jan 5th)

**Deliverable:**
Get the power measurements for the basic mode of the operation of QS222 in the non schedule mode.
QAM16, DR\_scale: 0; packet size : N/A.
Need to get a basic estimate of the order (like mA, uA,..) of the power consumption by QS222 for CES event.
Full in-depth studies of the power measurements across various operating modes (QAM/ Dr\_Scale/ packet sizes) will be done later and it will be tracked in a separate task.

tasks.name: N3: Baseband Testing - Schedule mode bring up
tasks.description:
**Debug Note – Sync Slot Reception Issue**
During sync slot operation, RX was failing to receive packets.
Root cause identified: **rxphy\_rx\_cfg\_sync\_address** was not aligned with the TX-side destination address. Since sync slot reception depends on address matching, RX will only accept packets when **rxphy\_rx\_cfg\_sync\_address** equals the TX destination address. Updated the register configuration accordingly to ensure proper packet reception

tasks.name: N3: LDO TEST
tasks.description:
Test description:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-bf74d86d-fefa-4077-a54f-ccae2050dc0a)
Deliverable:
1. bring out adc (QS\_VDD\_ADC), dac ( pcb pin name - QS\_VDD\_DAC), fe (pcb pin name - QS\_VDD\_FE), rxpll (QS\_VDD\_RXPLL), txpll (pcb pin name - QS\_VDD\_TXPLL) ldo using ana mux . and measure the voltages.
2. meausre these ldo ldo voltages from their respective pins.
3. vary the load from 100u to 1mA in step of 100uA for adc , de ldo and plot the load regulation curve
4. vary the load from 10-200uA in sep of 50uA for dac , rx pll and tx pll ldo. and get the load regulation curve
5. tx buffer ldo is 2V ldo. the output will come directly at QS\_VDD\_2P5 pin on the pcb. vary the current from 100uA to 3mA in step of 100uA and get the load regulation curve.

Risk: T- If it does not function as intended, we may need to proceed with a re-tapeout.
Limitation: Assumption - the setup is ready

2. Technical: Delays in obtaining detailed PCB layout and component allocation for QS_VDD_ADC, QS_VDD_DAC, QS_VDD_FE, QS_VDD_RXPLL, and QS_VDD_TXPLL.

tasks.name: Wearable bringup in QAM16 mode.

comments.text: Wearables were shown to be working fine. The headers are in the wrong order in the Python / FW, but Kumaresh has that lined up in his tasks. We had shown 1800 byte packets work with ECC in IDC at > 5cm distance. However, the BLE communication between the Python and Tx wearable fails with big packets because teh BLE can't keep up, which will be debugged by the Wearable folks
comments.author: Vinayak Agrawal

tasks.blockerReason:
# Delay Reason @pm:
1. Summary: The wearable bringup in QAM16 mode was delayed due to issues with BLE communication between the Python and Tx wearable.
2. Technical: Inadequate packet handling and transmission rates in the BLE protocol caused the system to fail with large packets.

raw.name: Wearable bringup in QAM16 mode.

comments.text:
@Vinayak Agrawal  - Here are the followup items as per discussion in the silicon results review meeting :

• Double check whether the start sequence inverted version is being given properly to avoid packet rejection. ~Vinayak Agrawal
 
• Bring out the signal from internals as well as internal modules to see what is happening there. ~Vinayak Agrawal
 
• Capture the match item regarding the data sampling and polarity inversion signal from RXD. ~Vinayak Agrawal
 
• Run experiments to check the inverted bits and ensure the setup for 100% PMDR debugging is correct. ~Vinayak Agrawal
 
• Utilize the available QAM 16 data to improve EVM results. ~Vinayak Agrawal
 
• Run 100 for the PDFP experiments as suggested and then move over to COM 16. ~Vinayak Agrawal
 
• Sample INQ data using SRAM with the symbol clock.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

tasks.name: N3: [Misc_test] TEMPARATURE SENSOR TEST
tasks.description:
**Why:** Ixana's QS222 - have a temp sensor that needs to be validated for the proper functioning of the QS222 product.
**Deliverable:** Measure the temperature analog value and digital code in analog and digital path
**Risks (T,E):** Risk: T- If it does not function as intended, we may need to proceed with a re-tapeout.
**Limitations: (Assumptions(A), Constraints (C), Dependency )**
C: Constraint: Since Ixana post silicon lab doesn't have means to test at the other temperatures - it is assumed that the temp sensor readings from QS222 at room temp is good enough indicator for the working of the temp sensor.
A: Assumption- Setup and config are correct

**Required documents for reference:**
Test Description:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-641deb0a-acf8-4d5d-9f5b-988569c5aee9)
Temp sensor doc: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/p/shovan/EWB4HZ7\_FdZEp-bL0etlDC8B7lSGQhQydC7ssU1qgrlIOA?wdOrigin=TEAMS-MAGLEV.p2p\_ns.rwc&wdExp=TEAMS-TREATMENT&wdhostclicktime=1763450236605&web=1](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/p/shovan/EWB4HZ7_FdZEp-bL0etlDC8B7lSGQhQydC7ssU1qgrlIOA?wdOrigin=TEAMS-MAGLEV.p2p_ns.rwc&wdExp=TEAMS-TREATMENT&wdhostclicktime=1763450236605&web=1)

**Pre-si sim results**: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/p/shovan/ESvPrctczTlBuS6TWG4Nc4MB4LiTr\_6hltKclhnTO68nyw?e=aVa5lo](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/p/shovan/ESvPrctczTlBuS6TWG4Nc4MB4LiTr_6hltKclhnTO68nyw?e=aVa5lo)

**Design Contact**: Anirudhh.

Test Results: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/Temperature%20Sensor/Temperature%20Sensor.pptx?d=w05403ba2e8a241d687fb3628790ec925&csf=1&web=1&e=sJgCDY](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/Temperature%20Sensor/Temperature%20Sensor.pptx?d=w05403ba2e8a241d687fb3628790ec925&csf=1&web=1&e=sJgCDY)

comments.text:
Results are as expected - for the analog voltage measurement. Voltage decreases / increases as temp increases /decreases respectively. Need design input from @Aniruddh CHoudhary  for the ADC code output . 

If additional debug is needed for the ADC code for temp sense readout - a new task will be created.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
Hi Sarat, the pre-Si ADC output is specified for 25degreeC chip top temperature. The test environment has ambient temperature ~28degreeC. So, the chip-top temperature can be 10-20degrees higher than the ambient temperature. Also, the ADC reference is set to 0.503V but the simulation was done with 0.496V. Hence the lower ADC output.

The chip temperature was raised with the help of a hot air blower in the lab. The ADC code value decreased as the temperature increased and reduced back to the initial value once it cooled down to room temperature. This is the expected trend.
comments.author: Deepthi Kammath

comments.text:
Thanks Deepthi. ADC output is faraway from the expected value from pre-si. 

Voltage measurement seems a bit reasonable. 

When you say the code decreased with increase in temp, how did you raise the temp?
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Task Completed and results updated in the description
comments.author: Deepthi Kammath

comments.text: Added all details of why, deliverable, required documents, pre-si sim results, design contact.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

tasks.blockerReason:
Delay Reason @pm:
1. Summary: The temperature sensor test was delayed due to insufficient ADC code output matching pre-si simulation results.
2. Technical: Insufficient detail in thread regarding the exact ADC code value and its relation to chip-top temperature.

tasks.name: N3: [RX Validation] RX validation (PMDR studies)  with different carrier frequencies.
tasks.description:
**Why:** Need to characterize the RX across different carrier frequencies.

**Deliverable:** Get the PMDR plots across all combinations of carrier frequencies across QAM modes (first pass - if something is not expected - move to the other combinations) .

**Assumptions:** Hope the FW scripts from pre\_release work as expected and no surprises.

Results PPT: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Testing/RX\_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=zg02ZN&nav=eyJzSWQiOjgwOCwiY0lkIjoxOTU1MzkzNDY1fQ](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Testing/RX_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=zg02ZN&nav=eyJzSWQiOjgwOCwiY0lkIjoxOTU1MzkzNDY1fQ)
Summary: Except for QAM16 dr scale 0 and dr scale 1, for all other cases, the sensitivity is degraded for 17.5MHz and 12.5MHz compared to 20MHz carrier. However, the sensitivity improves with dr scale in all cases.

| QAM | dr scale | Sensitivity in uV |
| ---| ---| --- |
| 20M | 17.5M | 12.5M |
| 16 | 0 | 350 | 370 | 450 |
| 16 | 1 | 300 | 320 | 370 |
| 16 | 2 | 290 | 270 | 316 |
| 16 | 3 | 250 | 200 | 223 |
| 4 | 0 | 120 | 142 | 146 |
| 4 | 1 | 100 | 110 | 124 |
| 4 | 2 | 77 | 95 | 100 |
| 4 | 3 | 60 | 73 | 88 |
| 2 | 0 | 90 | 102 | 110 |
| 2 | 1 | 75 | 80 | 88 |
| 2 | 2 | 55 | 67 | 72 |
| 2 | 3 | 45 | 60 | 68 |

comments.text: Thanks @Deepthi Kammath  . Awesome job. Task completed.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Hi @Sarat Anumula, the tests are complete and the results are updated in the description. Can you please mark this task as complete?
comments.author: Deepthi Kammath

comments.text:
@Deepthi Kammath

Feedback from 26-Mar silicon results meeting:

With new GUI  / pre_release ; issues surfaced - sensitivity is not matching the expectations.

Hence decided to debug the issue with the GUI / pre_release settings - and hence this task is on hold and created a new task for the debug.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
Hi @Sarat Anumula, please find the status of this task:

Data collection is complete for 20MHz carrier across QAMs and dr scales.
We are getting 100% PMDR for 17.5MHz and 12.5MHz carriers. This is being debugged currently.

Thank you.
comments.author: Deepthi Kammath

tasks.name: N3: [RX power] QAM2, QAM4 - rerun for outlier measurements from previous runs.
tasks.description:
**Why:** Ixana wants to publish the data sheet for QS222 and hence needed to complete the RX power validation.

**Deliverable:** Get the power measurements for the outlier cases from the earlier task where the power measurements are taken for QAM2, QAM4 mode. The outlier cases are due to insufficient measurement window where the packet is present. Hence we need to get the correct data captured for the datasheet.

**Assumptions:** Hope the instruments and the data collection scripts work as expected for these outlier cases as well.

Results PPT: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Testing/RX\_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=XjNUUN&nav=eyJzSWQiOjc4MCwiY0lkIjoxNzcyMTU3MDA5fQ](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Testing/RX_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=XjNUUN&nav=eyJzSWQiOjc4MCwiY0lkIjoxNzcyMTU3MDA5fQ)

comments.text:
Continuous Improvements :
  Need to find a optimal way to calculate RX power because of the measurement
  timing - where the packet transmission is active at times the measurement window timing was such that there is no packet transmission.

Need to have a separate discussion to implement any changes from these learnings into QS127M.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:

raw.name: N3: [RX power] QAM2, QAM4 - rerun for outlier measurements from previous runs.
raw.description:
**Why:** Ixana wants to publish the data sheet for QS222 and hence needed to complete the RX power validation.

**Deliverable:** Get the power measurements for the outlier cases from the earlier task where the power measurements are taken for QAM2, QAM4 mode. The outlier cases are due to insufficient measurement window where the packet is present. Hence we need to get the correct data captured for the datasheet.

**Assumptions:** Hope the instruments and the data collection scripts work as expected for these outlier cases as well.

Results PPT: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Testing/RX\_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=XjNUUN&nav=eyJzSWQiOjc4MCwiY0lkIjoxNzcyMTU3MDA5fQ](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Testing/RX_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=XjNUUN&nav=eyJzSWQiOjc4MCwiY0lkIjoxNzcyMTU3MDA5fQ)
raw.creator: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
Continuous Improvements :
  Need to find a optimal way to calculate RX power because of the measurement
  timing - where the packet transmission is active at times the measurement window timing was such that there is no packet transmission.

Need to have a separate discussion to implement any changes from these learnings into QS127M.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

tasks.name: N3: [RX Power] QAM16 - schedule mode.
tasks.description:
**Why:** Need to get the RX power consumption in the active state.

**Deliverable:** Get the power measurements for the RX during the active state. Max throughput.
Phase 1: pathclear for the power measurements with DR\_Scale 0.

**Risks (T,E)**: N/A

**Limitations:** (Assumptions(A), Constraints (C), Dependency (D)) :
A: Assumption is that the FW works as expected.
A: Assumption is that for the max throughput - the config settings doesn't break anything.

Results PPT: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Testing/RX\_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=ozUeMb&nav=eyJzSWQiOjc1NSwiY0lkIjo0ODkyNjM5NDl9](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Testing/RX_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=ozUeMb&nav=eyJzSWQiOjc1NSwiY0lkIjo0ODkyNjM5NDl9)

comments.text: Thanks @Deepthi Kammath  for the notes. Due date changed to 6-Mar. Let me know if you need any help from design or any other team for the ongoing debug.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
Hi @Sarat Anumula, this task is in progress, we were able to resolve the 2 issues which were seen before.

The reverse trend in Voltage vs Current: This was due to the higher LDO output voltage setting and hence the 1p2 input voltage sweep was not sufficient for the LDO to saturate. After lowering the LDO output voltage, the results are as expected.
The abrupt fluctuations seen in current numbers(100-150uA): This got resolved for 1p2 current numbers after setting the LDOs and Blocks, for ADC and FE; to be ON all the time. 0p8 numbers is yet to be looked at.

With the above resolutions, I tried getting some baseline numbers for the FE and ADC blocks, but the numbers are not aligning expectations. This is being debugged now. 

Can you please change the due date for this task for this Friday?
comments.author: Deepthi Kammath

comments.text: New task added for Deepthi. Details published as well.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
Feedback received from 12-Jan Silicon results review meeting. : 

Check with 
Different SPI freq. 
Different tie interval. 
On chip  -power supply. 
Double check all the other pin config settings and make sure they are at default settings ?
 
Make sure we enable the set up close to the System configuration. 
 
Current working setup (PMDR spike resolved) is with :
External supply. 
Front End forced, 
PLL forced.

tasks.description:
**Deliverable :** Complete one debug cycle.
**Debug Cycle :**
Get the test results (pass or fail) with the below set of parameters for the test : Packet size \[16,64,100,200,1000,1800\];
Dr scale = \[0 to 6 in step of 1\]
rs\_crc enabled & rs\_crc disabled as well.
**Assumption:** Current definitions of the packet interval timings in firmware work as expected and good. If not - then further debug is required.

**Results /Observations :**

tasks.name: QAM16 Rx testing with Frequency Offset sweep
tasks.description:
AIM: Test the Rx PMDR for QAM16 across various TX carrier frequency offsets ranging from -200ppm to 200ppm in steps of 20ppm. This is done to find the best frequency offset at which Rx performance is best and hence estimate the frequency offset of the DUT

Test results PPT: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Rampup/RX\_Ramp%20up.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=Zu6ZKo&nav=eyJzSWQiOjU4OSwiY0lkIjoyMDQ5Mzc4OTN9](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Rampup/RX_Ramp%20up.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=Zu6ZKo&nav=eyJzSWQiOjU4OSwiY0lkIjoyMDQ5Mzc4OTN9)
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/38746078-f72e-463f-b712-463433b3dc02/image.png)
